medications.name: dextroamphetamine-amphetamine (Adderall XR)
medications.notes: AM dose. Hold 24-48 hrs before surgery due to cardiovascular effects.

medications.name: dextroamphetamine-amphetamine (Adderall IR)
medications.notes: Two IR doses in the afternoon. Hold 24-48 hrs before surgery.

medications.name: Auvelity (dextromethorphan / bupropion)
medications.notes: ANESTHESIA FLAG: DXM component interacts with ketamine-based agents. Bupropion lowers seizure threshold and can raise blood pressure. Must flag for all surgical/procedural teams.

medications.name: tirzepatide (compounded)
medications.dose: current dose per NW Compounders protocol
medications.notes: SURGERY FLAG: Must hold minimum 1 week before any procedure due to gastric emptying effects and aspiration risk. Do NOT have surgery during active weight loss phase. Compounded; not the brand-name Mounjaro 5mg dose anymore.

medications.name: fludrocortisone
medications.notes: Monitor blood pressure carefully given newly diagnosed hypertension (Feb 2026). Not for POTS — for dysautonomia.

medications.name: vibegron (Gemtesa)
medications.notes: Recently switched FROM mirabegron (Myrbetriq). Do NOT list mirabegron as current. Do not restart mirabegron.

medications.name: loratadine
medications.notes: Part of dual H1+H2 antihistamine MCAS protocol.

medications.name: naltrexone LDN (compounded)
medications.notes: SURGERY FLAG: Must hold before surgery for adequate opioid-based post-operative pain management. Confirm hold timeline with prescriber. Pending possible increase to 4.5 mg per Dr. Brian Little.

medications.name: scopolamine patch
medications.purpose: Nausea prevention during Spravato (esketamine) treatment sessions

medications.name: benzocaine-lidocaine-tetracaine compound cream
medications.notes: Apply 40 minutes prior to procedure. Disp 5g, 3 refills. Related to hEDS local anesthetic resistance.

medications.name: famotidine (Pepcid AC)
medications.notes: Daily MCAS protocol — H1 (loratadine) + H2 (famotidine) dual stack. Currently OTC. Attempting to get prescribed through OHP via MyChart request.

medications.name: Hibicleanse (chlorhexidine gluconate 4%)
medications.purpose: Hidradenitis Suppurativa (HS) prevention — posterior/backs of thighs
medications.notes: HS well-controlled on this regimen. Also relevant as MRSA carrier — flag for all surgical teams.

medications.name: esketamine nasal spray (Spravato)
medications.notes: Sessions timed to allow DBT group shortly after for neuroplasticity window. Clinic: Active Path Mental Health. NMDA-related — coordinate with anesthesia if surgery planned.

medications.name: Slow Fe iron supplement
medications.notes: Ferritin trend: 15.1 (2019) → 36.1 (2020) → 97 (Mar 2025). Significantly improved on this regimen.

conditions.name: Hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome (hEDS)
conditions.notes: Primary connective tissue disorder. Accounts for joint instability, chronic pain, local anesthetic resistance. HIGH local anesthetic failure rate (Schubart 2019: ~88%). All procedures require MAC or general anesthesia — no local-only sedation.

conditions.name: Mast Cell Activation Syndrome (MCAS)
conditions.notes: Daily H1+H2 antihistamine protocol. Elevated anesthetic reaction risk — requires pre-medication protocol (H1 + H2 antihistamine + corticosteroids before procedural induction). Avoid known MCAS triggers.

conditions.name: Hidradenitis Suppurativa (HS)
conditions.notes: Active near posterior/backs of thighs. Significantly improved with ~80 lb weight loss. Flares now rare. Flag for any surgical team operating near these sites. MRSA carrier status relevant.

conditions.name: Chronic interstitial cystitis (IC)
conditions.notes: Onset 2017-06-02. Managed at NW Urology. Cystoscopy 2025-02-06.

conditions.name: Urge incontinence of urine
conditions.notes: New patient NW Urology Dec 2024. Bladder scan 2024-12-19.

conditions.name: C-PTSD (Complex Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder)
conditions.notes: Active DBT-based IOP; beginning to apply DBT skills automatically. Stellate ganglion blocks also indicated for C-PTSD (currently inaccessible due to insurance/provider dispute). Legal matter ongoing with victim advocate Slava.

conditions.name: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)
conditions.notes: Adult diagnosis. Severe sensory processing differences. Requires written communication preferences and hospital-grade sedation for all procedures. Procedural anxiety is severe. Document explicitly on all intake forms.

conditions.name: Dysautonomia
conditions.notes: Autonomic reflex testing 2022-10-04 at OHSU — abnormal study, excessive HR response during tilt (58→99 bpm). Cardiologist cleared Feb 2026; stated POTS resolved. Do NOT use POTS language. Still on fludrocortisone. ANESTHESIA FLAG: hemodynamic instability risk — careful fluid and vasopressor management required.

conditions.name: Hypertension, unspecified
conditions.notes: Newly diagnosed Feb 2026. Treatment plan not yet established. Monitor carefully given fludrocortisone and Auvelity (bupropion component can raise BP).

conditions.name: Positive ANA (antinuclear antibody) with polyarthralgia
conditions.notes: ANA positive July 2025 and December 2025. Follow-up bloodwork reportedly came back fine per Amber but full results not in records. Ordered by Dr. Lauren Fisher; referred to rheumatology. Titer and pattern not documented.

conditions.name: Sleep disorders: UARS, OSA, chronic insomnia, hypersomnia, circadian rhythm delay (DSPD)
conditions.provider: Pacific Sleep Program Portland (Dr. Radhika Breaden)
conditions.notes: Diagnostic polysomnogram 2023-10-11. No CPAP or active treatment in records.

conditions.name: Cataplexy
conditions.notes: Listed at July 2023 sleep clinic visit alongside hypersomnia, UARS, OSA, and circadian rhythm disorder. Narcolepsy status unclear.

conditions.name: PCOS (polycystic ovarian syndrome)
conditions.notes: Documented but without adequate monitoring or hormonal workup. No testosterone, LH/FSH, or fasting insulin on record.

conditions.name: GERD
conditions.notes: Pantoprazole and omeprazole tried and discontinued.

conditions.name: Anxiety disorder
conditions.provider: La Clinica Del Valle Family HC

conditions.name: Hypermobility syndrome
conditions.notes: Coded separately from hEDS in HealthEx records.

conditions.name: MRSA carrier (methicillin-resistant Staph aureus)
conditions.notes: Active in records. No decolonization protocol documented. Flag for ALL surgical teams. Relevant to HS and surgical planning. Decolonization may be needed pre-operatively.

conditions.name: Genital herpes simplex
conditions.notes: No suppressive antiviral in current medication list.

conditions.name: Prediabetic fasting glucose
conditions.notes: Fasting glucose 112 mg/dL (Aug 2024). No HbA1c on record anywhere. POC glucose 105 at same visit. Follow-up not documented.

conditions.name: Elevated WBC with neutrophilia
conditions.notes: WBC 18.44 with 85% neutrophils (Aug 2024 ER, during abdominal pain visit). Follow-up CBC status unclear. May have been done via Wolfpack/LabCorp (not connected).

conditions.name: Plantar fascial fibromatosis
conditions.notes: No current treatment.

conditions.name: TMJ (temporomandibular joint disorder)
conditions.notes: No current treatment.

conditions.name: Hyperhidrosis
conditions.notes: Researched; Botox treatment being considered. Under evaluation for Bangkok 2027 trip.

conditions.name: Vitamin D deficiency
conditions.notes: 15.6 ng/mL (Feb 2019 — deficient). 44.6 ng/mL (Feb 2020 — borderline). Recheck overdue.

conditions.name: Low serum ferritin (iron deficiency)
conditions.notes: Ferritin trend: 15.1 (2019) → 36.1 (2020) → 32 (2022) → 30 (2023) → 41 (2024) → 97 (Mar 2025). Significantly improved on Slow Fe.

conditions.name: Dextroscoliosis (thoracolumbar)
conditions.notes: Mild to moderate degree, noted on chest X-ray 2016-02-08. No further workup in records.

conditions.name: History of right distal radius fracture (comminuted intra-articular) with ORIF + hardware
conditions.provider: Dr. Michael D. Hwang (OHSU Orthopedics Tigard)
conditions.notes: Fracture 2020-09-29. ORIF surgery 2020-10-16 (plate + 8 screws). Extensor tendon rupture of right hand with repair 2020-12-22. Multiple post-op visits. Hardware in place.

conditions.name: History of foreign object rejection
conditions.notes: Flag for any implants, mesh, or anchors in future surgeries.

conditions.name: Chronic pain syndrome
conditions.notes: RFA at L4/L5/S1 indicated; currently inaccessible due to insurance/provider dispute.

conditions.name: Sleep-disordered breathing
conditions.notes: Noted in rheumatology visit.

allergies.notes: Confirmed. Onset 2023-03-08. Flag for all procedures and surgical environments.
Confirmed. Onset 2014-06-06. Use paper or silicone-based tape alternatives.

procedures.name: ORIF right distal radius (comminuted intra-articular fracture)
procedures.reason: Comminuted intra-articular distal radial fracture with displacement
procedures.outcome: Plate and 8 screws placed. Normal alignment restored. Healed with routine follow-up through June 2021.
procedures.notes: Hardware in place. Anesthesia: bupivacaine, lidocaine, fentanyl, ondansetron, Zofran, acetaminophen pre-op. Scopolamine patch pre-op. Multiple oxycodone PRN post-op. Hospitalization: same day. First surgery at Alberty Surgical Center.

procedures.name: Transfer of extensor tendon, right hand
procedures.reason: Extensor tendon rupture of right hand
procedures.outcome: Procedure completed. Multiple weeks of occupational therapy follow-up.
procedures.notes: Anesthesia agents used: bupivacaine, lidocaine, fentanyl, hydromorphone, meperidine, ondansetron, promethazine, cefazolin, LR. Post-op OT at OHSU Rehabilitation, Tigard through mid-2021.

procedures.notes: Referred by Dr. Brian Little. Medications withheld pre-test: Doxazosin 5 days, Propranolol 1 day, Zoloft 5 days.

anesthesia_flags.implication: ~88% local anesthetic failure rate (Schubart 2019). Standard local-only sedation will not work.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: MAC (Monitored Anesthesia Care) or general anesthesia required for ALL procedures. Document on all pre-procedure intake forms.

anesthesia_flags.implication: Risk of hemodynamic instability under anesthesia.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Careful fluid and vasopressor management required. Communicate to anesthesia team in advance.

anesthesia_flags.implication: Risk of anaphylactoid or mast cell-mediated reaction to anesthetic agents.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Pre-medication protocol required before induction: H1 antihistamine + H2 antihistamine + corticosteroids. Avoid known MCAS triggers.

anesthesia_flags.condition: Autism Spectrum Disorder — severe procedural anxiety
anesthesia_flags.implication: Sensory processing differences; standard pre-procedure communication insufficient.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Hospital-grade sedation required. Written communication preferences. Document explicitly on all intake forms.

anesthesia_flags.implication: DXM component interacts with ketamine-based anesthetic agents. Bupropion lowers seizure threshold.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Flag for ALL anesthesia teams. Discuss specific agents with prescriber. Bupropion may also elevate BP.

anesthesia_flags.implication: Will block opioid-based post-operative pain management if not held.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Must hold LDN before surgery. Confirm hold timeline with prescriber (Dr. Brian Little).

anesthesia_flags.implication: Aspiration risk if not held before surgery.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Hold tirzepatide minimum 1 week before any procedure. Do not schedule surgery during active weight loss phase.
Hold 24-48 hours pre-procedure. Plan cognitively for surgery prep without medication.

anesthesia_flags.implication: Risk of surgical site infection and transmission.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Flag for all surgical teams. Decolonization protocol may be needed pre-operatively.

anesthesia_flags.implication: Risk of rejection of implants, mesh, anchors, or hardware.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Flag for any procedure involving implantable materials.

anesthesia_flags.implication: Active HS near potential surgical/positioning sites.
anesthesia_flags.action_required: Flag for any procedure requiring positioning near posterior thighs.
Use leg elevation and hot pack on arm. Severe procedural anxiety — document explicitly. Allow extra time and staffing.

appeals_and_disputes.subject: Radiofrequency Ablation (RFA) L4/L5/S1 — access and financial assistance dispute
appeals_and_disputes.against: Providence Health Assurance / Health Share of Oregon (CareOregon CCO)
appeals_and_disputes.notes: Core issue: Dr. Stapleton moved RFA from PPMC (where PFA covered 100% facility cost) to East Portland Surgery Center and NW Spine & Laser Surgery Center, which do not honor Providence Financial Assistance. Upfront demand: $3,116.19. Amber is on SSI/OHP. Formal grievances filed with OHA Ombuds and Providence Grievance on 2026-03-04. Health Share response letter (2026-03-03) acknowledged facts but offered no resolution. Contradictory statements: said both 'PPMC no longer allowing RFA' AND 'procedure not approved by Plan.' Follow-up escalation email sent 2026-03-13 with 10-day deadline. No response by 2026-03-25. Formal appeal v2 drafted 2026-03-25. Legal angles: ADA Title II, Section 1557 ACA, Section 504, OAR 410-141-3515, network adequacy. New provider options from nurse (2026-03-25): Pain Care Specialists and Columbia Pain Management — need to verify if they do all 3 procedures + sedation + hospital setting + PFA. CareOregon contact: Wendy (503-416-4100).

appeals_and_disputes.subject: HHS OCR Complaint — ADA/Section 504/Section 1557 re: pain management access

insurance.name: Oregon Health Plan (OHP)
insurance.notes: Primary coverage. Providence Financial Assistance (PFA) at 100% for Providence facilities. PFA does NOT apply to East Portland Surgery Center or NW Spine & Laser Surgery Center.

insurance.name: Providence Financial Assistance
insurance.notes: 100% coverage for facility fees at Providence-affiliated facilities. Does not extend to independent surgery centers. Central to RFA access dispute.
